summary.intent: Refactor the gossamer desktop app (an Entire.io/Claude Code session viewer) into a VS Code extension to eliminate install friction, with a monorepo structure separating core indexing logic from the UI layer.
summary.outcome: The monorepo was scaffolded with packages/core (extracted indexing/DB logic) and packages/vscode (extension + React/Mantine webview). The extension activates on .entire/settings.json detection, spawns the gossamer server as a child process, and renders the sessions screen — though the server startup/communication was still being debugged at session end.

repo: Entire.io writes session data to a dedicated git branch (not the DB directly); gossamer reads that branch and indexes it into SQLite via Prisma/better-sqlite3
Entire.io presence is detectable via .entire/settings.json at the repo root — no git commands needed for detection
The gossamer server (dist/serve.js) must be pre-built before the VS Code extension can spawn it as a child process
The existing Electron app uses HTTP/WebSocket API calls in app/src/renderer/src/api.ts — this pattern was reused for the webview rather than switching to postMessage
Prisma client output path in packages/core is ../prisma/... relative to src/ (one level up, not two)

code.finding: Server is spawned using system node (looked up via 'which node' shell command) rather than process.execPath, which points to VS Code's bundled Electron Node.js — critical to avoid native module NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch
Vite output must be loaded with type='module' on the script tag; omitting it causes the webview to silently render blank
root must use an absolute path (__dirname + '/src/webview') — relative paths resolve from the cwd of wherever vite build is invoked, not the package directory

entryFileNames/chunkFileNames/assetFileNames must be set to fixed names (index.js, index.css) to match the hardcoded paths in GossamerPanel.ts's HTML template; default Vite hashed filenames break this

workflow: VS Code extension development: F5 with a launch.json preLaunchTask triggers build then opens an Extension Development Host window; Cmd+R reloads it after changes
Extension host stdout/stderr appears in the Debug Console (View → Debug Console), not the Output panel — easy to miss

better-sqlite3 (and Prisma by extension) cannot be imported directly into the VS Code extension host due to NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch; the workaround is spawning a child process with system Node.js
When migrating to npm workspaces, the -w flag uses the 'name' field from package.json, not the directory name
The gossamer server does a schema push on startup that takes several seconds; the webview needs to wait for a readiness signal before fetching data

friction: Prisma path was initially set two levels up (../../prisma) but should be one level up (../prisma) from packages/core/src/ — caught only at build time

VS Code's bundled Node.js has a different NODE_MODULE_VERSION than system Node.js, making direct Prisma/better-sqlite3 import impossible in the extension host; required rearchitecting to spawn a child server process
Vite's default content-hashed output filenames (index-DvZdMzP5.js) broke the hardcoded asset paths in GossamerPanel.ts — required adding explicit entryFileNames config
Vite build fails with 'Cannot resolve entry module index.html' when run from repo root with -w flag due to relative root path in vite.config.ts
Webview rendered blank with no error due to missing type='module' on the script tag — silent failure with no console output
Server startup race condition: webview fetched sessions before the server finished its schema push, producing 'error loading sessions'
open_items: Wire the real sessions screen data flow end-to-end — server startup logging and the debug loop was still in progress at session end
Electron app (app/) is intentionally broken/abandoned but not deleted — should be cleaned up once the VS Code extension is stable
CLI entrypoint for packages/core deferred — module API only for now, CLI packaging to revisit later
Only the main sessions screen was ported; detail screens, event viewer, and other Electron screens not yet migrated to the webview
better-sqlite3 native binary needs to be rebuilt for VS Code's Node.js version if direct import is ever needed (currently sidestepped by child process approach)
Optional background daemon for capturing events when VS Code is closed — deferred to post-v1
VS Code Marketplace publishing process not yet addressed
